currentState: Broken pair still visible in live drill. Chapter 13 keeps Lyriel present only as the right-side absence Cyndia checks before she looks at the living again.
powerDynamic: The old symmetry is gone, but it still shapes movement. Lyriel remains the missing measure against which Cyndia unconsciously sets the lane.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Chapter 9 opens with the old left-right structure intact: Cyndia ranging left, Lyriel holding right and rear.
After the trap, the relationship survives only as negative space in Cyndia's repeated right-side checks.
Chapter 11 shows the relationship only as absence-in-motion: Cyndia checks the empty right at the chapter open and again on the final northbound road.
The right-side check appears on the Darrowmere shore before the crossing and again at the breach window before Cyndia takes in who has actually come back.

nameUsage.cyndiaCallsLyriel: No new direct naming on page; the relationship survives as habitual motion rather than speech.
nameUsage.lyrielCallsCyndia: Only present through the remembered shape of the old formation.

history.event: By chapter 9 the two women operate as an experienced flank pair inside the four-ranger formation.
Lyriel dies in the compound trap, permanently breaking the pair and rewriting Cyndia's movement habits.
The chapter confirms Lyriel’s death is still rewriting Cyndia’s movement one road later; the pair remains active in continuity as scar tissue, not memory speech.
Chapter 13 confirms Lyriel's absence still lives in Cyndia's body even during a different mission on different ground.

trajectory: Working flank pair -> sudden permanent rupture -> absence embedded in surviving drill across multiple chapters.

stateHistory.currentState: Broken pair still visible in live drill. Lyriel is gone, but chapter 11 proves the partnership survives as Cyndia’s repeated right-side checks and the formation memory both riders still carry.
